models.name: LSTM
models.purpose: Temporal prediction of server load and power
models.interpretation: Highest sequential modelling complexity but materially higher energy demand than tree-based alternatives.

models.name: Random Forest
models.interpretation: Stable nonlinear baseline with moderate energy cost and solid predictive behaviour.

models.name: XGBoost
models.interpretation: Strong balance of predictive quality and low energy demand, making it highly suitable for orchestration loops.

models.name: Extended XGBoost
models.purpose: Improved prediction using extended telemetry features
models.interpretation: Best overall accuracy and stability across the evaluated telemetry-driven models.

features.name: Fan speed
features.description: Cooling effort proxy used for thermal-energy coupling.

features.name: Inlet temperature
features.description: Thermal boundary condition at system intake.

features.name: Outlet temperature
features.description: Thermal response indicator after workload execution.

features.name: Ambient conditions
features.description: Room-level environmental context affecting cooling behaviour.

outcome_statement: AI-based telemetry modelling enabled predictive energy modelling without requiring a physical water-cooling testbed. The results support energy-aware workload orchestration in constrained data-centre environments.
